message:
feat(workflows/runner): tick 181 per-step loop cx.checkpoint seam (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Fourth tick of the workflows/runner.rs strangler-fig. Adds the
per-iteration cancellation seam to the step loop inside
`run_workflow_inner`.

New cancellation seam (top of each loop iteration):
- When `cx: Option<&Cx>` is `Some`, runs `cx.checkpoint()` before
  any per-step work.
- On cancel, performs the FULL cleanup sequence that every other
  abort-during-execution path uses (fail_execution +
  mark_trigger_event_handled + lock_manager.release +
  record_workflow_terminal_action) so the pane lock and trigger
  state don't leak under a cancelled parent.
- Returns `WorkflowExecutionResult::Error` with a "cancelled at
  step N" error message (N = current step index). The step index
  in the message is useful for debugging which iteration saw the
  cancel.

When cx is None (legacy caller), the seam is a no-op — byte-for-
byte identical execution.

This is the biggest single cancellation surface in the workflow
runner: long-running workflows with many steps (e.g. 50+ step
escalation workflows, or workflows with wait-for policies) could
previously block indefinitely under an upstream cancel. Now each
step-boundary is an observable cancellation point.

Cleanup cost note: ~40 lines of inline cleanup. A future refactor
tick could extract this into a `cancel_and_abort_workflow` helper
that's shared with the plan-validation-error path (which has the
same cleanup sequence). Deferred — the extraction pattern is
clearer once tick 182 finishes migrating per-step-branch storage
calls.

Verified via fork bypass: 31/31 workflows::runner:: tests pass.
The per-step cx.checkpoint is currently exercised only by
run_workflow_with_cx callers (none yet) — contract test deferred
until tick 183 wires up the callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
